name: INTRAMURAL AORTIC HEMATOMA (3-13%
definition: ATYPICAL AORTIC DISSECTION = IMH aortic dissection without demonstrable intimal flap / penetrating aortic ulcer
discriminating_features: NO intimal tear / flap at initial imaging
DIAGNOSTIC eccentric cuff / crescent of aortic wall > 7 mm thick with a density of 60-70
attenuation identical to intraluminal blood after 1 week
decreased thickness within a few weeks

general: NO intimal tear / flap at initial imaging
DIAGNOSTIC eccentric cuff / crescent of aortic wall > 7 mm thick with a density of 60-70
attenuation identical to intraluminal blood after 1 week
decreased thickness within a few weeks
displacement of intimal calcifications maintaining a semicircular / circular curvilinear
CECT: nonenhancing mural region of low attenuation with smooth border maintaining a constant
MR: subacute / chronic hematoma on T1 black blood imaging:
tiny intimal orifice ± connection with intercostal / lumbar artery
